message:
test: add distance-measures + chordal/tree/core fuzz target

fuzz_distance_measures drives the BFS-heavy distance algorithms in
fnx_algorithms together with the chordal / tree / core-decomposition
families that I just built conformance harnesses for, so structural
panics or invariant breaks on adversarial graph topologies surface
in continuous fuzzing rather than user reports.

Functions exercised:

- distance_measures (eccentricity + diameter + radius + center +
  periphery in one BFS sweep) — with three runtime invariants that
  cross-check the summary fields against the per-node vector:
    * diameter == max(eccentricity)
    * radius == min(eccentricity)
    * center ⊆ argmin(eccentricity)
    * periphery ⊆ argmax(eccentricity)
  on connected non-empty inputs. Any drift between the summary and
  the per-node vector triggers a fuzz crash.
- wiener_index, wiener_index_directed, harmonic_diameter
- barycenter (with non-empty-result invariant on connected inputs)
- is_chordal, chordal_graph_treewidth (must error gracefully on
  non-chordal input, never panic)
- is_tree, is_forest
- core_number, k_core, k_truss, onion_layers (k bounded to a small
  modulus so the fuzzer doesn't park on the empty-result branch)

Smoke-tested with 5000 runs across two passes (1857 cov features,
no crashes, no invariant breaks). Seeded with 14 hand-crafted seeds
covering every DistanceInput variant (with the variant-tag byte set
so each seed dispatches to a different branch) plus 8 representative
discoveries from the smoke run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
